description:
You are an Industrial Engineer optimizing semiconductor yield using the FAB_ANALYTICS schema in Oracle (connected as fab_admin/Yield2024 to XEPDB1). Analyze defect coordinates on silicon wafers to identify equipment failures and calculate yield metrics.

The analysis requires the creation of three views. `WAFER_DEFECT_STATS_VW` joins WAFERS and DEFECTS to calculate `wafer_id`, `lot_id`, `wafer_number`, `total_defects`, `max_defect_distance` (SQRT((x_coord - center_x)^2 + (y_coord - center_y)^2)), `edge_defect_pct` (percentage of defects with distance from center >= 90% of radius_mm), and `r_squared` (REGR_R2(y_coord, x_coord), treating NULL as 0). `WAFER_CLASSIFICATION_VW` classifies failure signatures by evaluating criteria in order: 'EDGE_RING' (edge_defect_pct >= 60 and total_defects > 50), 'SCRATCH' (r_squared >= 0.80 and total_defects > 30), or 'RANDOM' (all others), and sets an `is_scrap` flag (1) if total_defects > 100 or the signature is 'EDGE_RING' or 'SCRATCH'. `LOT_YIELD_SUMMARY_VW` aggregates results by `lot_id` to show `total_wafers`, `scrap_wafers`, and `yield_pct` (rounded to 2 decimals). Export the lot yield summary, ordered by yield_pct ascending, to /home/ga/Documents/exports/lot_yield_report.csv.
